choice: lib/tracking.ts (DB) + lib/tracking-math.ts (pure, tested). Layer 1 = the active Plan's latest frozen Forecast bands. Layer 2 actual = net-worth trajectory: at each distinct snapshot date, sum each asset's latest balance as-of that date (carry-forward), falling back to manualValue; net worth by bucket uses each asset's latest balance/manualValue. Achieved percentile = piecewise-linear interpolation of today's net worth against the Plan band's 5 points (p10/p25/p50/p75/p90) for the current year, extrapolated beyond p10/p90. Layer 3 re-forecast = runForecast over buildEngineInput(plan, currentYear), i.e. 10k trials seeded from TODAY's actual balances under the Plan's assumptions, giving forward bands + the headline successProbability. Staleness = days since most recent snapshot. The dashboard headline success uses the re-forecast (not the frozen Plan) so it stays current with actuals. Pure/DB split keeps the math unit-testable.
rationale: [build-originated] 5edebac0 specified the three layers conceptually; this captures the computation contracts: carry-forward net-worth trajectory, 5-point percentile interpolation, re-forecast = engine over current snapshots under Plan assumptions, headline tracks the re-forecast, and the pure/DB split for testability.
